Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.json5l

Class Json5lTokenReader

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.JsonTokenReader
org.apache.juneau.marshall.json5.Json5TokenReader
org.apache.juneau.marshall.json5l.Json5lTokenReader
All Implemented Interfaces:
Closeable, AutoCloseable, RecordReader, TokenReader
public class Json5lTokenReader extends Json5TokenReader
Reference implementation of the public TokenReader surface for the JSON5L format.

Subclasses Json5TokenReader to inherit the JSON5 dialect relaxations (single-quoted and bare-identifier strings/field names, trailing commas, missing values, comments) and adds JSONL's flat top-level sequencing: each line's value is emitted in turn at depth 0 and JsonTokenReader.next() returns TokenType.END_OF_STREAM only when the input is exhausted.

Notes:
  • This class is not thread safe.
  • The cursor is a true O(1)-memory streaming cursor (TokenReader.isStreaming() == true).

  • Constructor Details

    • Json5lTokenReader

      public Json5lTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe) throws IOException
      Constructor with default settings.
      Parameters:
      pipe - The parser input pipe to read from. Must not be null.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying reader.

    • Json5lTokenReader

      public Json5lTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe, JsonTokenReader.Settings settings, Json5lParserSession session) throws IOException
      Constructor used by Json5lParserSession.parseTokens(Object) to plumb the calling session through so that JsonTokenReader.read(Class) can delegate to the JSON5L databind path.
      Parameters:
      pipe - The parser input pipe. Must not be null.
      settings - The cursor-level settings. Must not be null.
      session - The Json5lParserSession for JsonTokenReader.read(Class) delegation, or null to disable read.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying reader.

  • Method Details

    • afterValue

      protected void afterValue()
      Description copied from class: JsonTokenReader
      Sets the post-value state based on the enclosing container.

      Subclasses (e.g. JsonlTokenReader) override this when the format treats consecutive top-level values differently — JSONL transitions back to JsonTokenReader.S00_expectValue at depth 0 instead of JsonTokenReader.S05_end so the next line is read.

      Overrides:
      afterValue in class JsonTokenReader
